ksa_text: Ability to align strategy, business objectives, and technology capabilities, integrating strategic vision, business priorities, and technical capabilities into a unified organizational direction.

dcwf.top_element_label: Engage and collaborate with allies and partners to advance shared strategic AI objectives.

nist_ai_rmf.top_element_label: Policies, processes, procedures, and practices across the organization related to the mapping, measuring, and managing of AI risks are in place, transparent, and implemented effectively.

top_anchors.element_label: Policies, processes, procedures, and practices across the organization related to the mapping, measuring, and managing of AI risks are in place, transparent, and implemented effectively.
Engage and collaborate with allies and partners to advance shared strategic AI objectives.

pass_2_evaluation.rationale: Leadership & Strategy is a primary or secondary domain for the Data and AI Transformation Lead. This ability KSA is within scope for the role's expected competency profile and seniority level (senior specialist).

pass_3_adversarial_internal.ruling: No internal inconsistency identified. Domain scope (Leadership & Strategy), seniority fit, and signal level are internally consistent with inclusion.

pass_4_adversarial_external.challenge: Pass 1 signal is moderate (composite 0.455). External reviewers may question whether this specific ability KSA is sufficiently distinctive for the Data and AI Transformation Lead versus adjacent roles.
pass_4_adversarial_external.evidence: McKinsey, Gartner, and Harvard Business Review consistently identify leadership and strategy skills as core differentiators for C-suite and VP data leaders.
pass_4_adversarial_external.ruling: Upheld: moderate framework signal is consistent with Leadership & Strategy appearing in job postings and role definition literature for Data and AI Transformation Lead.

decision_rationale: Leadership & Strategy ability KSA is within scope for the Data and AI Transformation Lead at the senior specialist tier. Seniority fit is appropriate. All three evaluation passes support inclusion.
